upper.title: Narrative Tenses, Used to, Would
upper.content:
📌 Past Simple – Sự kiện chính trong câu chuyện
Dùng để kể các sự kiện theo trình tự thời gian (main events of a story).

• When she opened the door, she pretended we weren't there.
• He called me and asked me to pick him up.

Cũng dùng để nói về thói quen hoặc trạng thái trong quá khứ:
• We often went to the bar before dinner.
• He really liked sport and was very fit.
📌 Past Continuous – Dựng bối cảnh câu chuyện
Dùng để thiết lập bối cảnh (set the scene) hoặc hành động đang diễn ra bị gián đoạn:

• Last night, I was walking home when…
• The sun was shining, and lots of tourists were lying on the beach. Suddenly…
• When she opened the door, we were talking about her.
📌 Past Perfect Simple – Sự kiện xảy ra trước
Dùng để nói về sự kiện xảy ra trước sự kiện chính (earlier past):

• When she opened the door, he had already left.
• I realised that I had been there before.

Dùng với động từ chỉ trạng thái (stative verbs) để diễn tả khoảng thời gian:
• The day Anne died, they had been married for 48 years.

Dùng để nói bao nhiêu / bao nhiêu lần đã làm gì trước đó:
• We had driven 500 miles, and we needed some rest.
📌 Past Perfect Continuous – Hành động kéo dài trước sự kiện chính
Dùng với dynamic verbs để diễn tả hành động kéo dài liên tục hoặc lặp đi lặp lại trước thời điểm quá khứ:

• I was very angry. I had been waiting for him in the cold.
• We had been driving for less than an hour when the car broke down.
• She had been writing a letter every day for over a year.
📌 Used to + infinitive – Thói quen & trạng thái cũ
Dùng cho hành động lặp lại hoặc trạng thái không còn đúng ở hiện tại:

• When I was a child, I used to go skiing every winter.
• As a child, I used to be blond.
• I used to live with my mum when I was a child.
• I didn't use to smoke before I got this job.
📌 Would + infinitive – Thói quen lặp lại (KHÔNG dùng cho trạng thái)
Dùng cho hành động lặp đi lặp lại trong quá khứ. KHÔNG dùng cho trạng thái (stative verbs):

• Every Saturday we would go to the shopping centre with my parents.
• My teacher would tell me to shut up at the beginning of every class.

❌ I would be blond as a child. → ✅ I used to be blond as a child.
⚠️ Lỗi thường gặp
❌ When I arrived, she was leaving already. → ✅ she had already left (hoàn tất trước)
❌ I would live in Paris as a child. → ✅ I used to live in Paris (trạng thái dùng used to)
❌ He used to going → ✅ He used to go (used to + bare infinitive)
I was walking home when I suddenly remembered that I had left my keys at the office.Past Cont. (bối cảnh) + Past Simple (sự kiện) + Past Perf. (trước đó)
As a child, I used to spend summers at my grandparents' farm. We would wake up early and help with the animals.Used to (trạng thái/thói quen) + Would (thói quen lặp lại)

exercises.q: By the time the rescuers reached the climbers, they ___ (wait) on the ledge for nearly twelve hours.

exercises.q: She ___ (never/travel) abroad before, so she was very nervous at the airport.

exercises.q: When I was at school, my best friend and I ___ (walk) home together every afternoon.
exercises.exp: Would / Used to: thói quen lặp lại trong quá khứ

exercises.q: The neighbours ___ (argue) so loudly that I could hear every word from my flat.
exercises.exp: Past Continuous: hành động đang diễn ra tại thời điểm quá khứ

exercises.q: He ___ (not/use/be) interested in politics, but working abroad changed his perspective completely.
exercises.ans: didn't use to be / used not to be
exercises.exp: Used to phủ định: trạng thái không còn đúng ở hiện tại (stative verb)

writing_prompts.vi: Kể lại một câu chuyện về một chuyến đi đáng nhớ, sử dụng đầy đủ các thì quá khứ (past simple, past continuous, past perfect) để dựng bối cảnh và kể sự kiện.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Narrative tenses: Past Simple, Past Continuous, Past Perfect Simple & Continuous
writing_prompts.example: Last summer, I was travelling through the mountains of northern Vietnam when I suddenly noticed that I had taken the wrong path. I had been walking for nearly three hours and hadn't seen another person. While I was trying to find my bearings, it started to rain heavily. I realised I had left my map at the hotel. By the time I found a small village, it had been raining for over an hour and I was completely soaked. The villagers, who had never seen a foreigner in that area before, welcomed me warmly and offered me shelter.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả cuộc sống của bạn hoặc một người thân khi còn nhỏ, so sánh thói quen và cuộc sống ngày xưa với hiện tại, sử dụng 'used to' và 'would'.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Used to + infinitive vs. Would + infinitive (past habits & states)
writing_prompts.example: When I was a child, my family used to live in a small town in the countryside. We didn't use to have a television, so we would spend evenings listening to the radio together or playing board games. My grandmother would cook enormous meals every Sunday and the whole family would gather around the table. I used to be afraid of the dark and would always sleep with a small lamp on. Life used to be much simpler then — we didn't use to worry about social media or mobile phones. Things have changed enormously since those days.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một đoạn văn kể lại một sự kiện quan trọng trong lịch sử hoặc một tin tức bạn đã đọc, sử dụng kết hợp các thì tường thuật để trình bày sự kiện rõ ràng.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Narrative tenses combined: sequence of past events
writing_prompts.example: It emerged last year that a group of scientists had been working on a revolutionary medical treatment for over a decade. By the time they announced their results, they had already conducted hundreds of clinical trials. While researchers were presenting their findings, journalists from around the world were waiting outside the conference hall. The team revealed that they had been collaborating with universities in five different countries, and that the breakthrough had come unexpectedly while they were testing a completely different compound. Many people who had been suffering from the condition for years finally had reason to hope.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một ngày bạn đã có kế hoạch nhưng mọi thứ không diễn ra như mong đợi — mọi thứ đã xảy ra trước đó (past perfect) ảnh hưởng đến kế hoạch của bạn.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Past Perfect & Past Continuous for narrative complications
writing_prompts.example: I had been looking forward to the concert for weeks. I had bought the tickets months in advance and had already planned the whole evening. However, when I arrived at the venue, I discovered that the organisers had already cancelled the show — apparently, the main artist had fallen ill that morning. It was raining heavily and I had forgotten to bring an umbrella. I had also left my phone at home, so I couldn't contact my friends. The evening I had planned so carefully ended up being one of the most chaotic nights of my life, though I did laugh about it later.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về tuổi thơ của bạn hoặc của một nhân vật hư cấu — kết hợp dựng bối cảnh, thói quen cũ, và sự kiện quan trọng đã thay đổi mọi thứ.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Mixed narrative tenses + used to / would
writing_prompts.example: As a child, Maria used to dream of becoming a musician. She would practise the piano for hours every afternoon, and her parents would sit quietly in the next room listening. She had never imagined that one day she would perform on a real stage. Then, one afternoon while she was practising a particularly difficult piece, a famous composer who had been visiting the town heard her playing through the open window. He knocked on the door and what happened next changed her life completely. By the time she turned eighteen, she had already performed in three countries. She would never forget the moment everything changed.

upper.title: Future Forms: Expressing Future Time
upper.content:
📌 Will – Quyết định tức thời & Dự đoán
Instant decisions (quyết định ngay lúc nói):
• 'We don't have sugar.' 'Don't worry, I'll buy some.'

Predictions (dự đoán dựa trên suy nghĩ/quan điểm):
• I think he'll win the election.

Offers, promises, refusals, requests:
• I'll carry that bag for you. (offer)
• Will you open the door? (request)
• I won't lend him my car. (refusal)
📌 Be going to – Ý định & Dự đoán có bằng chứng hiện tại
Intentions/plans (ý định chưa sắp xếp cụ thể):
• I'm going to play tennis with Elisabeth today.

Predictions with present evidence (thấy bằng chứng rõ ràng):
• Don't drive like that. We're going to have an accident!
• The doctor said I'm going to have a girl.
📌 Present Continuous – Kế hoạch đã sắp xếp sẵn
Dùng khi đã xác định ngày giờ/địa điểm cụ thể (sắp xếp, hẹn hò):

• I'm seeing the dentist at 6.
• We are getting married next week.
• I'm flying to New York tomorrow morning.
📌 Present Simple – Lịch trình & Thời gian biểu
Dùng cho các sự kiện theo lịch cố định (timetables, schedules):

• The train leaves at 4.
• Shops close at 6.
• I have my yoga class tomorrow at 10.
📌 Future Continuous – Hành động đang diễn ra ở tương lai
• Sự kiện đang xảy ra tại một thời điểm cụ thể trong tương lai:
This time next week, we'll be travelling to Paris.

• Hỏi lịch sự về kế hoạch:
Will you be going home this summer?

• Kế hoạch đã sắp xếp (thay cho Present Continuous):
We'll be leaving at 8 a.m. tomorrow.
📌 Future Perfect – Hoàn tất trước một thời điểm tương lai
Future Perfect Simple (will have + V3): hành động hoàn tất trước mốc tương lai:
• By 2050, researchers will have found a cure for cancer.
• By this time next year, I'll have graduated.

Future Perfect Continuous (will have been + V-ing): thời gian kéo dài đến mốc tương lai:
• By the end of the year, she will have been working on the publication for over ten years.
📌 Future Time Clauses
Sau when, as soon as, until, before, after → dùng Present Simple (KHÔNG dùng will):

• I'll retire when I'm 70. ❌ when I'll be
• I won't call you until I arrive. ❌ until I will arrive
📌 Shall – Đề nghị & Gợi ý
• Shall I = đề nghị làm gì cho ai:
Shall I organise the meeting?
• Shall we = gợi ý:
Shall we eat out today?
He looks very pale. He's going to faint!Be going to: dự đoán có bằng chứng nhìn thấy
By the time she retires, she will have been teaching for 40 years.Future Perfect Continuous: thời gian đến mốc tương lai

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả kế hoạch cho một sự kiện lớn trong tương lai (đám cưới, chuyến đi, buổi ra mắt sản phẩm), sử dụng nhiều dạng thì tương lai khác nhau.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Mixed future forms: will, be going to, Present Continuous, Future Continuous, Future Perfect
hint_words: by that time we will have
this time next month we will be
writing_prompts.example: We are getting married in September and we have already booked the venue and the caterer. I am going to choose my dress next weekend — I've been looking at designs for months. I think the ceremony will be beautiful because we've planned every detail carefully. This time next year, we will be celebrating our first anniversary. By the time the reception starts, most of our guests will have been travelling for several hours, so we want to make sure there's plenty of food and entertainment. As soon as the ceremony finishes, the photographer will take pictures in the garden.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về kỳ vọng và mục tiêu của bạn trong 5 năm tới, kết hợp dự đoán, ý định và các mốc thời gian.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Will (predictions), be going to (intentions), Future Perfect, Future Continuous
hint_words: this time next year I will be
writing_prompts.example: By the time I am thirty, I will have completed my postgraduate degree and hopefully launched my own business. I am going to apply for a scholarship next month — I've been preparing the application for weeks. I think the field of renewable energy will grow enormously in the next decade, which is why I want to specialise in that area. This time next year, I will probably be living in a different city. Once I have enough experience, I will set up my own consultancy. By then, I will have been working in the industry for at least three years.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn là một nhà báo. Viết một bản tin ngắn về các sự kiện sắp xảy ra trong tuần tới, dùng nhiều dạng tương lai.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Future forms in formal/news contexts: will, Present Simple (schedules), Future Continuous
hint_words: by the end of the week
writing_prompts.example: The international climate summit begins on Monday in Geneva, where delegates from over 150 countries are meeting to discuss carbon reduction targets. World leaders will be arriving throughout the day on Sunday. The talks are expected to last five days, and by Friday, negotiators will have reviewed proposals from every major economy. The president is going to deliver the opening address at 10 a.m. sharp. Many observers think an agreement will be reached, but some analysts believe the most difficult negotiations will only begin on day three, when specific targets come under discussion.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một đoạn hội thoại giữa hai người bạn đang lên kế hoạch cho một buổi tối ra ngoài, dùng đề nghị, gợi ý, và các hình thức tương lai.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Will (instant decisions, offers), Shall (suggestions), Present Continuous (arrangements), Be going to
writing_prompts.example: Shall we go out for dinner on Friday? I know a great new Italian place that opened last month. — Sounds good! What time does it close? — I think it stays open until midnight, but I'll check their website. Shall I book a table for seven o'clock? — Yes, please do. I'm going to finish work early that day, so I'll meet you outside the office at half six. Will you be bringing anyone else? — No, it's just the two of us. Oh, and I won't be drinking tonight — I'm driving. I'll order sparkling water.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một thành phố trong tương lai — dự đoán cách mà công nghệ, giao thông và môi trường sẽ thay đổi.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Will (predictions), Future Perfect, Future Continuous, Be going to
writing_prompts.example: By 2050, self-driving cars will have replaced most traditional vehicles in major cities. Governments are already going to invest billions in redesigning road infrastructure. Citizens will be using a combination of electric buses and high-speed rail for most of their daily journeys. It is likely that many office buildings will have been converted into green spaces or residential housing as remote work continues to grow. By that time, solar energy will have become the dominant power source, and cities will be generating more electricity than they consume. Pollution levels will have dropped dramatically, and urban populations will be living longer, healthier lives.

upper.title: Other Ways to Express Future
upper.content:
📌 Be about to + infinitive – Sắp xảy ra ngay
Dùng để nói điều gì đó sẽ xảy ra trong tương lai rất gần:

• Some apps are about to disappear from the market.
• Scientists say they are about to find a vaccine.
• Hurry up! The film is about to start.
📌 Be on the brink/verge/point of + -ing – Sắp xảy ra (sắp đến mức nguy kịch/quan trọng)
• Our economy is on the brink of collapse.
• They are on the verge of becoming world champions.
• The two enemies are on the point of reaching an agreement.

💡 Thường dùng cho sự kiện quan trọng, trọng đại, hoặc nguy hiểm.
📌 Be due to + infinitive – Dự kiến/Theo lịch
Dùng cho sự kiện được lên kế hoạch hoặc dự kiến xảy ra:

• Greece is due to repay the loan next semester.
• The secretary is due to arrive in Montreal tomorrow morning.
• The train is due to depart at 14:30.
📌 Be to + infinitive – Trang trọng & Chỉ thị
Tin tức/Sự kiện chính thức:
• Prince William is to visit Paris next week. (=It is expected)
• Nine care homes are to close by March.

Chỉ thị/Lệnh chính thức (formal instructions):
• All employees are to attend the orientation.
• You are not to leave this room. (prohibition)

Trong mệnh đề if (điều kiện):
• If we are to avoid repeating mistakes, we must act now.
• If he is to succeed, he must work harder.
📌 Be bound to + infinitive – Chắc chắn sẽ xảy ra
Dùng khi điều gì đó gần như chắc chắn sẽ xảy ra:

• They are bound to like him. He's so charming.
• His new film is bound to win the hearts of audiences.
• If you don't study, you are bound to fail.
📌 Be likely/unlikely to + infinitive – Có/Không có khả năng
Be likely to: dự đoán có thể xảy ra:
• The government is likely to pass new regulations soon.
• It's likely that the company will pay damages.

Be unlikely to: ít có khả năng xảy ra:
• He is unlikely to win this match.
• It's unlikely that the weather will change.
⚠️ So sánh nhanh
• Be about to = ngay bây giờ, rất sắp
• Be due to = theo lịch, dự kiến
• Be to = tin tức chính thức / chỉ thị
• Be bound to = chắc chắn xảy ra
• Be likely to = có khả năng xảy ra
• On the verge/brink/point of = sắp đến mức (thường quan trọng/nghiêm trọng)
The company is on the verge of announcing a major merger.On the verge of: sắp xảy ra sự kiện quan trọng
The prime minister is to visit the disaster zone tomorrow.Be to: tin tức chính thức, ngôi 3

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn là phóng viên. Viết một bản tin về một sự kiện chính trị quan trọng sắp xảy ra, sử dụng be to, be due to, be bound to và be likely to.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Be to (official), be due to (scheduled), be bound to (certainty), be likely to (prediction)
hint_words: the vote is due to take place
observers say it is likely to
negotiations are on the verge of
a statement is due to be issued
writing_prompts.example: The prime minister is to hold a crucial emergency summit next Thursday, where leaders from the G7 nations are due to discuss the ongoing energy crisis. The meeting is bound to attract significant media attention, given the urgency of the situation. Analysts say the talks are likely to produce a joint statement, though observers warn that a binding agreement is unlikely to be reached in a single session. According to government sources, a formal announcement is due to be made before the end of the week. If world leaders are to address the crisis effectively, they will need to demonstrate unprecedented unity.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết đoạn văn về một nhân vật trong câu chuyện đang đứng trước một khoảnh khắc quan trọng — sắp đưa ra quyết định hoặc hành động lớn. Dùng be about to, on the brink of, on the verge of.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Be about to, on the brink of, on the verge of (imminent events in narrative)
hint_words: he stood on the brink of
they were on the verge of
just as she was about to
the crowd was on the point of
he realised he was about to make
on the verge of giving up
writing_prompts.example: Clara stood backstage, her heart pounding. She was about to walk onto the stage and perform in front of five thousand people. After years of hard work, she was on the verge of achieving everything she had ever dreamed of. She had been on the brink of giving up countless times, but something had always pushed her forward. Just as she was about to step out, her phone buzzed — it was a message from her mother. Everything was about to change, and she knew it. She took a deep breath and walked into the light.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một bức thư trang trọng từ ban giám đốc đến nhân viên, thông báo các thay đổi sắp tới trong công ty. Sử dụng be to (chỉ thị), be due to (theo kế hoạch), be likely to, be bound to.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Be to (formal instructions), be due to (scheduled), be likely to / bound to (predictions)
hint_words: the new policy is due to

writing_prompts.example: Dear Team, We are writing to inform you of important upcoming changes to our organisation. The restructuring process is due to begin on 1st June, and all staff are to attend a briefing session during the week of 27th May. Employees are not to discuss details of the changes with external parties until the official announcement has been made. We recognise that this transition is bound to cause some uncertainty, and we want to reassure everyone that support will be available. The new structure is likely to create greater efficiency across all departments. If the company is to remain competitive, these changes are essential. Further details are due to be shared at next Monday's all-hands meeting.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một vận động viên hoặc nghệ sĩ đang trên đỉnh cao sự nghiệp, sử dụng be bound to, be likely to, be on the verge of để nói về tương lai của họ.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Be bound to, be likely to, be on the verge of (strong predictions and near-future)
hint_words: he is on the verge of
critics say she is likely to
is on the brink of breaking
writing_prompts.example: At just twenty-three years old, Rafael is on the verge of becoming one of the most celebrated tennis players of his generation. After winning three consecutive titles this season, he is bound to enter the world's top five rankings by December. Critics say his aggressive playing style is likely to cause problems for even the most experienced opponents. writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một cuộc khủng hoảng môi trường và các biện pháp cần thiết, sử dụng đa dạng các cách diễn đạt tương lai gồm be to, be on the brink of, be due to, be likely to, be unlikely to.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Mixed future expressions: be to, be on the brink of, be due to, be likely / unlikely to
hint_words: the ecosystem is on the brink of
writing_prompts.example: Several critically endangered ecosystems are on the brink of irreversible collapse, according to a new report released this week. Governments around the world are to meet at an emergency summit next month, where new international agreements are due to be signed. Experts warn that dozens of species are likely to disappear within the next decade without urgent intervention. It is unlikely that voluntary commitments alone will be sufficient — binding targets are now essential. If the world is to prevent the worst consequences of biodiversity loss, this summit is bound to be a defining moment. The report makes clear that this loss is likely to affect food security and water supplies for billions of people. Activists say it is high time world leaders acted rather than simply issued statements.

upper.title: Future in the Past
upper.content:
📌 Future in the Past – Tương lai nhìn từ quá khứ
Khi kể chuyện trong quá khứ và muốn đề cập đến điều sẽ xảy ra (tương lai) tại thời điểm đó, chúng ta dùng dạng quá khứ của các thì tương lai.

Nguyên tắc: Đổi sang dạng quá khứ tương ứng
📌 Bảng chuyển đổi Future → Future in the Past
Future (hiện tại)Future in the Past
will + Vwould + V
be going to + Vwas/were going to + V
Present Continuous (future)Past Continuous
Present Simple (timetable)Past Simple
Future Continuouswould be + V-ing
Future Perfectwould have + V3
📌 Would – Tương lai trong quá khứ (chung)
• I knew you would fail the exam. You didn't study at all!
• She promised she would call me when she arrived.
• He asked whether I would help him.
📌 Was/Were going to – Kế hoạch/Ý định trong quá khứ (thường không thực hiện)
• Sorry, I was going to call you today, but I forgot. (có ý định nhưng không làm)
• I was going to buy the car, but I decided not to.
• He was going to be sent to Iraq, but he fell ill.
📌 Past Continuous – Kế hoạch đã sắp xếp trong quá khứ
• That night I was very excited because the next morning I was going back home.
• I lost my passport the day before we were leaving for our honeymoon.
📌 Would be + V-ing – Future Continuous in the Past
• I told him not to call me that evening, because I would be studying all night.
• She knew she would be working overtime that weekend.
📌 Be about to / Be due to / Be on the verge of – trong quá khứ
• He was about to leave when the phone rang.
• The train was due to arrive at noon.
• She was on the verge of giving up when she got the news.
⚠️ Lỗi thường gặp
❌ I knew you will fail. → ✅ I knew you would fail.
❌ She said she is going to call. → ✅ She said she was going to call.
❌ He lost his ticket the day before the flight will depart. → ✅ the flight was due to depart
She said she was going to apply for the job, but in the end she changed her mind.Was going to: ý định trong quá khứ không thực hiện
I knew that this moment would change everything for both of us.Would: dự đoán/biết trước trong quá khứ

exercises.q: The conference ___ (due/start) at 9 a.m. but was postponed by an hour.
exercises.exp: Was due to: theo lịch trong quá khứ

exercises.q: She ___ (about/sign) the contract when her lawyer called with urgent news.

exercises.q: She was on the verge of ___ (give up) when she finally got a positive response.
exercises.exp: On the verge of + V-ing: sắp bỏ cuộc trong quá khứ

exercises.exp: Would (not): lời hứa trong quá khứ (reported)

exercises.exp: Would be + V-ing: hành động dự đoán đang diễn ra trong tương lai (nhìn từ QK)

exercises.q: The athletes ___ (due/compete) in three events, but two were cancelled.
exercises.exp: Were due to: theo lịch trong quá khứ

exercises.q: He asked me where I ___ (stay) during the conference.
exercises.exp: Would be + V-ing: Future Continuous in the past (indirect question)

exercises.q: Little did she know that this decision ___ (change) the course of her life.
exercises.exp: Would: irony/narrative device – tương lai trong quá khứ

exercises.q: They ___ (going to/announce) the winner that evening, but the event ran late.
exercises.exp: Were going to: kế hoạch bị trì hoãn trong quá khứ

exercises.q: Nobody realised that it ___ (be) the last time they would all meet together.
exercises.exp: Would be: nhận thức muộn về điều đã xảy ra trong tương lai từ quá khứ

exercises.q: She told me she ___ (call) back, but I never heard from her again.
exercises.exp: Would: tương lai trong quá khứ (reported speech / intention)

exercises.q: We ___ (about/leave) for the airport when we realised we had forgotten the passports.
exercises.exp: Was/were about to: hành động sắp xảy ra nhưng bị ngăn lại

exercises.q: He didn't realise that the decision he made that day ___ (change) his life forever.
exercises.ans: was going to change / would change
exercises.exp: Was going to / Would: hệ quả tương lai nhìn từ thời điểm quá khứ

exercises.q: The match ___ (due/start) at three o'clock but was delayed because of the weather.
exercises.exp: Was due to: sự kiện được lên lịch trong quá khứ

exercises.q: She ___ (on the point/resign) when her manager offered her a better contract.
exercises.ans: was on the point of resigning
exercises.exp: Was on the point of + -ing: sắp làm điều gì đó tại thời điểm quá khứ

writing_prompts.vi: Kể lại một kỷ niệm quan trọng về một chuyến đi hoặc sự kiện — sử dụng future in the past để mô tả những gì bạn đã nghĩ hoặc lên kế hoạch tại thời điểm đó.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Would, was going to, was about to, would be + -ing (future in the past)
hint_words: I had no idea it would
we were leaving the next day
writing_prompts.example: The night before our expedition, we were all convinced it would be a straightforward climb. We were going to set off at dawn and reach the summit by midday — at least, that was the plan. I was about to go to sleep when I heard thunder in the distance, but I dismissed it. Little did I know that by the next morning, a severe storm would force us to turn back. I had been so excited because I thought this trip would be the highlight of my year. I had promised my friends I would take photos at the top. Instead, I would be telling a very different story when I returned home.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết lại một cảnh trong phim hoặc tiểu thuyết yêu thích của bạn, dùng future in the past để kể về những gì nhân vật nghĩ sẽ xảy ra.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Was going to, would, was about to, was due to, was on the verge of
hint_words: she had no idea what would happen
he was on the verge of
writing_prompts.example: In the final chapter, the detective was about to reveal the identity of the killer when the lights suddenly went out. He had planned the dramatic announcement carefully — it was going to be the moment that explained everything. Nobody in the room knew what was about to happen. The suspect was on the verge of escaping through the back door. Little did he realise that the detective had already called for backup, and officers were due to arrive at any moment. He thought he would be free, but in fact, the trap was about to close around him.

writing_prompts.vi: Dùng future in the past để kể về một kế hoạch kinh doanh hoặc dự án mà bạn từng có nhưng đã thay đổi hướng đi.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Was going to (abandoned plans), would (expectations), was due to, was about to
writing_prompts.example: Three years ago, my business partner and I were going to launch an app that would connect local farmers directly with restaurants. We had secured initial funding and the product was due to go live in the spring. We were about to sign a major contract with a restaurant chain when the pandemic changed everything. I had expected the project would generate revenue within six months, but we quickly realised it would need to be completely redesigned. We were going to give up entirely, but we decided we would pivot to a subscription model instead. That decision would turn out to be the best one we ever made.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một nhân vật lịch sử hoặc nhân vật hư cấu trong khoảnh khắc định mệnh, sử dụng future in the past để diễn tả sự kiện sắp xảy ra và hậu quả không ngờ.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Was about to, would, was going to, was due to, little did they know
hint_words: he had no idea it would
writing_prompts.example: On the morning of 28th June 1914, Archduke Franz Ferdinand was going to attend a series of official events in Sarajevo. His motorcade was due to pass through the city centre at eleven o'clock. Security was lighter than usual — nobody thought there would be any serious threat. He was about to continue his journey when his driver made a wrong turn. Little did anyone know that this small mistake would place him directly in the path of history. He had no idea that the events of that morning would trigger a chain reaction that would lead to the First World War, reshaping the world for generations.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một câu chuyện ngắn sử dụng tất cả các dạng 'future in the past': would, was going to, was about to, was due to, would be + V-ing.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: All future in the past forms combined in narrative
hint_words: she was about to give up
she thought she would be sitting
writing_prompts.example: Maya checked her watch. The train was due to arrive in ten minutes, and she was going to use the journey to rehearse her speech one last time. She thought she would be sitting comfortably in first class by now, but there had been delays all morning. She was about to call the conference organisers when she spotted a familiar face on the platform — her old mentor, Professor Singh. She knew it would be a surprise to see him there. He had promised her he would be watching her presentation remotely. Little did she know that this chance encounter on a freezing train platform would change the direction of her research entirely, and that everything she had prepared would be set aside in favour of a completely new idea.
